title: dalbergia nigra
intro: Dalbergia nigra, also known as Brazilian rosewood or black rosewood, is a tropical tree native to Brazil and other parts of South America. It is valued for its timber, which is highly prized for its durability and beauty, but also contains medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The bark and roots of the tree have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, offering benefits like reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving circulation. The herbal preparations of Dalbergia nigra include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are typically consumed as teas or applied topically to skin conditions like wounds and eczema.
